crafts.title: Origami Desk Organizer
steps: Gather 4 sheets of square recycled paper.
Fold each corner to the center.
Fold the top and bottom edges to the center.
Open up the flaps into a 3D box.
Connect multiple boxes to form an organizer.

crafts.title: Self-Watering Planter
steps: Cut a 2-liter plastic bottle in half.
Poke a small hole through the plastic cap.
Thread a cotton string through the cap hole to act as a wick.
Fill the bottom half with water, invert the top half into it.
Fill the top portion with soil and your plant!

crafts.title: DIY Bottle Bird Feeder
steps: Wash and dry a bottle completely.
Cut two holes near the bottom and slide a wooden dowel through.
Cut a slightly larger hole above the perch so seeds spill.
Fill with seed, hang from a tree!

crafts.title: Steampunk Jewelry Piece
steps: Carefully disassemble the back of the broken watch.
Mount the tiny brass gears onto a blank ring or necklace pendant.
Seal it with a clear resin to create custom, luxury steampunk jewelry.

crafts.title: Precision Cleaning Tool
steps: Boil your old toothbrush to completely sanitize it.
Hold the neck of the brush over a candle flame until soft.
Bend the head at a 90-degree angle. You now have a custom grout cleaning brush!

crafts.title: Plarn (Plastic Yarn)
steps: Cut off the handles and bottom seam of the plastic bag.
Cut the remaining tube into thick horizontal strips.
Loop the strips tightly into each other to form a giant continuous ball of 'Plarn'.
Crochet or knit this waterproof yarn into sleep mats or tote bags.

crafts.title: Ice Pack Swabs / Seed Starters
steps: Soak an old clean sponge in water and place it in a ziplock bag in the freezer for a cheap lunchbox ice pack.
Alternatively, place seeds into the holes of a damp sponge to rapidly sprout seedlings before planting them.

crafts.title: Succulent Teacup Gardens
steps: Fill an old chipped mug or teacup with 1 inch of pebbles for drainage.
Fill with cactus soil and plant small succulent cuttings.
Decorate your windowsill instantly!

crafts.title: Plant Markers
steps: Pull the ink tube out of the dead plastic pen.
Paint the empty plastic barrel a bright solid color.
Write the name of a plant on the side using a sharpie.
Stick the pen body straight into your garden soil to label your herbs!
